batches.title: Smoked Salmon & Avocado Prep
steps: Toast sourdough bread lightly (2 min at toaster)
Slice and portion into 7 containers
Portion smoked salmon into 7 containers (Diego 80g, Diana 60g)
Portion avocado quarters into 7 containers (Diego 1/4, Diana 1/8)
Store bread and salmon separately in airtight containers; keep avocado refrigerated with lemon juice
yields: 7 breakfast servings: sourdough toast, smoked salmon, avocado ready-to-assemble

day_of_assembly.Monday: Toast 1 slice sourdough 1 min, spread avocado, layer salmon, squeeze lemon, serve 500ml water
day_of_assembly.Tuesday: Toast 1 slice sourdough 1 min, spread avocado, layer salmon, squeeze lemon, serve 500ml water
day_of_assembly.Wednesday: Toast 1 slice sourdough 1 min, spread avocado, layer salmon, squeeze lemon, serve 500ml water
day_of_assembly.Thursday: Toast 1 slice sourdough 1 min, spread avocado, layer salmon, squeeze lemon, serve 500ml water

storage.container: airtight container on ice or coldest shelf
storage.safety_note: Fish spoils fast. Use within 2 days; 1 day is safer. Reheat to 74 °C.

batches.title: Mediterranean Grilled Chicken, Quinoa & Roasted Vegetables
steps: Rinse 2 cups dry quinoa under cold running water
Bring 4 cups salted water to boil, add quinoa, simmer 15 min until fluffy
Cool quinoa completely, divide into 7 airtight containers (Diego 180g, Diana 130g)
Pat dry 8 chicken breasts (1200g total), season with sea salt, pepper, oregano, thyme
Grill or bake chicken on 200°C oven for 20 min until internal temp 165°C (use meat thermometer)
Cool, slice, portion into 7 containers (Diego 200g per day, Diana 150g per day)
Chop 2 kg mixed vegetables (zucchini, bell peppers, carrots, cherry tomatoes)
Toss vegetables with 2 tbsp olive oil, sea salt, pepper, dried oregano
Roast at 200°C for 25 min, stirring halfway
Cool, divide into 7 containers (Diego 1.5 cups, Diana 1.2 cups)
yields: 7 days × lunch: 180g (Diego) or 130g (Diana) cooked quinoa + 200g (Diego) or 150g (Diana) sliced grilled chicken + 1.5 cups (Diego) or 1.2 cups (Diana) roasted vegetables

day_of_assembly.Monday: Reheat quinoa 2 min, reheat chicken 2 min, reheat veg 2 min, drizzle 2 tsp (Diego) or 1.5 tsp (Diana) olive oil, fresh oregano, serve 500ml water
day_of_assembly.Tuesday: Reheat quinoa 2 min, reheat chicken 2 min, reheat veg 2 min, drizzle 2 tsp (Diego) or 1.5 tsp (Diana) olive oil, fresh oregano, serve 500ml water
day_of_assembly.Wednesday: Reheat quinoa 2 min, reheat chicken 2 min, reheat veg 2 min, drizzle 2 tsp (Diego) or 1.5 tsp (Diana) olive oil, fresh oregano, serve 500ml water
day_of_assembly.Thursday: Reheat quinoa 2 min, reheat chicken 2 min, reheat veg 2 min, drizzle 2 tsp (Diego) or 1.5 tsp (Diana) olive oil, fresh oregano, serve 500ml water
day_of_assembly.Friday: Reheat quinoa 2 min, reheat chicken 2 min, reheat veg 2 min, drizzle 2 tsp (Diego) or 1.5 tsp (Diana) olive oil, fresh oregano, serve 500ml water
day_of_assembly.Saturday: Reheat quinoa 2 min, reheat chicken 2 min, reheat veg 2 min, drizzle 2 tsp (Diego) or 1.5 tsp (Diana) olive oil, fresh oregano, serve 500ml water

storage.safety_note: Cool quickly; never leave poultry at room temp > 2 h. Reheat to 74 °C through.

batches.title: Greek Yogurt, Walnuts & Berries Snack Prep
steps: Portion 1500g Greek yogurt into 7 containers (Diego 200g, Diana 150g)
Divide 175g walnuts into 7 small bags (Diego 25g, Diana 20g)
Keep yogurt refrigerated; store walnuts in cool, dry place
yields: 7 pm snack portions: Greek yogurt + walnuts pre-portioned

day_of_assembly.Monday: Spoon yogurt into bowl, top with walnuts and 100g (Diego) or 80g (Diana) fresh raspberries, serve 500ml water
day_of_assembly.Tuesday: Spoon yogurt into bowl, top with walnuts and fresh berries, serve 500ml water
day_of_assembly.Wednesday: Spoon yogurt into bowl, top with walnuts and fresh berries, serve 500ml water
day_of_assembly.Thursday: Spoon yogurt into bowl, top with walnuts and fresh berries, serve 500ml water
day_of_assembly.Friday: Spoon yogurt into bowl, top with walnuts and fresh berries, serve 500ml water
day_of_assembly.Saturday: Spoon yogurt into bowl, top with walnuts and fresh berries, serve 500ml water

storage.container: sealed original packaging or airtight container
storage.safety_note: Check use-by date on packaging. Opened yogurt: 5–7 days.

batches.title: Baked White Fish, Green Beans & Sweet Potato
steps: Pat dry 7 white fish fillets (160g Diego, 120g Diana), place on parchment paper, season with sea salt, wrap loosely, refrigerate
Blanch 1.5 kg green beans in boiling salted water 4 min, ice-bath immediately to stop cooking, drain, divide into 7 containers (Diego 200g, Diana 150g)
Peel & chop 1.2 kg sweet potato into 1-inch chunks, toss with 1 tbsp olive oil, sea salt, pepper
Roast sweet potato at 200°C for 25 min, stirring halfway, divide into 7 containers (Diego 150g, Diana 120g)
yields: 7 dinner servings: pre-portioned fish fillets + blanched green beans + roasted sweet potato

day_of_assembly.Monday: Preheat oven to 200°C, place fish on parchment, lemon juice, 0.5 tsp olive oil, sea salt, bake 12 min, reheat green beans & sweet potato 2 min each, plate, serve 500ml water
day_of_assembly.Tuesday: Preheat oven to 200°C, place fish on parchment, lemon juice, 0.5 tsp olive oil, sea salt, bake 12 min, reheat green beans & sweet potato 2 min each, plate, serve 500ml water
day_of_assembly.Wednesday: Preheat oven to 200°C, place fish on parchment, lemon juice, 0.5 tsp olive oil, sea salt, bake 12 min, reheat green beans & sweet potato 2 min each, plate, serve 500ml water
day_of_assembly.Thursday: Preheat oven to 200°C, place fish on parchment, lemon juice, 0.5 tsp olive oil, sea salt, bake 12 min, reheat green beans & sweet potato 2 min each, plate, serve 500ml water

storage.container: airtight container on ice or coldest shelf
storage.safety_note: Fish spoils fast. Use within 2 days; 1 day is safer. Reheat to 74 °C.

batches.title: Herb Omelette & Toast Breakfast Prep
steps: Slice 270g whole-grain bread into 7 slices, store in airtight container (will keep 3–4 days)
Chop fresh herbs (dill, chives, parsley) finely, store in small airtight container with paper towel to absorb moisture
yields: 7 breakfast portions: sliced whole-grain bread + fresh herb mix ready for omelette

day_of_assembly.Tuesday: Heat 1 tsp butter in non-stick pan, whisk 3 (Diego) or 2 (Diana) eggs + salt + fresh herbs, pour into pan, cook 3 min, fold, toast 1 bread slice 1 min, butter lightly, serve with 150g (Diego) or 120g (Diana) fresh strawberries, 500ml water

storage.safety_note: Add a splash of water before reheating to restore moisture. Use within 3 days.

batches.title: Turkey Meatballs, Brown Lentils & Roasted Beets
steps: Mix 600g ground turkey with 2 tbsp breadcrumbs, 1 egg, 1 tsp ground cumin, 0.5 tsp chili powder, 1 minced garlic clove, sea salt, pepper
Shape into 12 meatballs (approx 50g each), place on parchment-lined baking sheet
Bake at 200°C for 20 min until cooked through (internal temp 165°C)
Cool, divide into 7 portions (Diego 3 meatballs, Diana 2.5 meatballs)
Cook 1 cup dry brown lentils in 3 cups water + 1 tsp sea salt for 25 min until tender, cool
Divide into 7 containers (Diego 1 cup cooked, Diana 0.75 cup)
Roast 1.5 kg beets (peeled, sliced) at 200°C with 1 tbsp olive oil, sea salt, pepper for 30 min
Cool, divide into 7 containers (Diego 200g, Diana 150g)
yields: 7 lunch servings: 3 or 2.5 turkey meatballs + 1 cup (Diego) or 0.75 cup (Diana) cooked brown lentils + 200g (Diego) or 150g (Diana) roasted beets

day_of_assembly.Tuesday: Reheat 3 (Diego) or 2.5 (Diana) meatballs at 180°C 4 min, reheat lentils 2 min, mix with 2 tbsp fresh parsley + 1 tsp balsamic vinegar + 1 tsp olive oil, reheat beets 2 min, plate, serve 500ml water

storage.safety_note: Cool quickly; never leave poultry at room temp > 2 h. Reheat to 74 °C through.

batches.title: Hummus & Vegetable Crudités Snack
steps: Portion 560g hummus (store-bought or homemade) into 7 small containers (Diego 80g, Diana 60g)
Slice 700g carrots into sticks, 560g celery into sticks
Store in airtight container with damp paper towel to keep crisp
Halve 560g cherry tomatoes, store in separate container
yields: 7 pm snack servings: hummus + fresh vegetable sticks ready to serve

day_of_assembly.Tuesday: Spoon hummus into small bowl, arrange carrot sticks, celery, cherry tomatoes around, serve 500ml water

storage.safety_note: Cooked beans and lentils keep well. Freeze in portions for quick meals.

batches.title: Lean Ground Beef & Chickpea Taco Filling
steps: Brown 600g lean ground beef in large skillet over medium-high heat, breaking apart as it cooks (8 min)
Add 2 tsp ground cumin, 1 tsp chili powder, juice of 1 lime, sea salt, pepper, stir for 2 min
Cool, divide into 7 containers (Diego 150g, Diana 120g)
Cook 1.5 cups dry chickpeas (or drain/rinse 2 cans), portion into 7 containers (Diego 0.75 cup, Diana 0.6 cup)
Shred 1.5 kg red cabbage, store in airtight container with damp paper towel
Dice 2 bell peppers, store in separate container
Chop fresh cilantro, store in small container
yields: 7 lunch servings: seasoned ground beef + cooked chickpeas + prepped vegetables for taco bowls

day_of_assembly.Tuesday: Reheat 150g (Diego) or 120g (Diana) beef 2 min, reheat chickpeas 2 min, arrange in bowl with 1 cup (Diego) or 0.8 cup (Diana) shredded red cabbage + 0.5 cup (Diego) or 0.4 cup (Diana) diced peppers, top with cilantro, squeeze lime, warm 2 corn tortillas (Diego) or 1.5 (Diana) 30 sec/side, serve tortillas on side, 500ml water

storage.safety_note: Store in coldest part of fridge. Reheat to 74 °C before serving.

batches.title: Kefir Bowl with Granola & Walnuts
steps: Portion 1.75 L kefir into 7 containers (Diego 250ml, Diana 200ml), refrigerate
Portion 280g no-sugar granola into 7 small bags (Diego 40g, Diana 30g)
Portion 175g walnuts into 7 small bags (Diego 25g, Diana 20g)
yields: 7 breakfast servings: pre-portioned kefir + granola + walnuts

day_of_assembly.Wednesday: Pour kefir into bowl, top with granola, walnuts, fresh blueberries (100g Diego, 80g Diana), serve 500ml water

storage.container: sealed original packaging or airtight container
storage.safety_note: Check use-by date on packaging. Opened yogurt: 5–7 days.

batches.title: Pan-Seared Salmon, Wild Rice & Asparagus
steps: Cook 1.5 cups dry wild rice in 3.75 cups salted boiling water for 45 min until tender, cool, divide into 7 containers (Diego 200g, Diana 150g)
Pat dry 8 salmon fillets (170g Diego, 130g Diana), wrap individually in parchment, refrigerate
Blanch 1.75 kg asparagus in boiling salted water 3 min, ice-bath, drain, divide into 7 containers (Diego 250g, Diana 200g)
yields: 7 lunch servings: wild rice + raw salmon fillets ready to sear + blanched asparagus

day_of_assembly.Wednesday: Heat 1 tsp olive oil in non-stick pan over medium-high heat, pat salmon dry, sear skin-side down 4 min, flip, cook 3 min until opaque, reheat wild rice 2 min, reheat asparagus 2 min, plate salmon, rice, asparagus, fresh dill & lemon, serve 500ml water

storage.container: airtight container on ice or coldest shelf
storage.safety_note: Fish spoils fast. Use within 2 days; 1 day is safer. Reheat to 74 °C.

batches.title: Greek Yogurt with Pistachios Snack
steps: Portion 1.54 kg Greek yogurt into 7 containers (Diego 220g, Diana 170g)
Portion 175g pistachios into 7 small bags (Diego 25g, Diana 20g)
yields: 7 pm snack servings: Greek yogurt + pistachios pre-portioned

day_of_assembly.Wednesday: Spoon yogurt into bowl, top with crushed pistachios, serve 500ml water

storage.container: sealed original packaging or airtight container
storage.safety_note: Check use-by date on packaging. Opened yogurt: 5–7 days.
